title: Shinjis rejection of Misatos sexual invitation
display_title: Shinjis rejection of Misatos sexual invitation
wikitext:
[]: Shinji's rejection of Misatos "sexual" invitation             Quote Post by Leighton » Sun Mar 13, 2011 10:24 am Now that we've looked heavily on Shinji's point of view in the situation, lets have a look a Misato's pov Im guessing it was a mixture of both, but was she attempting to soothe, relieve, etc his trauma after Rei's suicide as a means of: being motherlike and taking care of him or with some sexual invitation to physically relieve him whilst possibly relieving him mentally? We know that Shinji most likely had no intention of rooting Misato, but what was HER intention?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Mar 13, 2011 10:43 am to have sex

[]:             Quote Post by Leighton » Sun Mar 13, 2011 11:05 am Xard wrote: to have sex

[]:             Quote Post by Ritsuko » Sun Mar 13, 2011 11:58 am Well during HIP when Misato remembers that part, everybody got disgusted with her. I just rewatched that scene on friday.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Mar 13, 2011 2:04 pm It was clear that she needed a cuddle -- whether it would be an "adult cuddle" or something more innocent is really less important -- as her interaction with Pen-Pen showed. Being stuck there to keep looking after the kids meant that she couldn't then swan off and descend upon her favourite bridge bunny for solace.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Mar 13, 2011 2:53 pm Mr. Tines wrote: It was clear that she needed a cuddle -- whether it would be an "adult cuddle" or something more innocent is really less important -- as her interaction with Pen-Pen showed. Being stuck there to keep looking after the kids meant that she couldn't then swan off and descend upon her favourite bridge bunny for solace.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Sun Mar 13, 2011 5:17 pm I think she wanted to give comfort but also to receive it.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Mar 13, 2011 5:18 pm TehDonutKing wrote: Once again, Misato hit on a friggin' penguin.

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Sun Mar 13, 2011 5:47 pm She says "All I can do is this . . ." and then puts her hand on Shinji's shoulder, only for him to flinch away. Call me crazy, but I think I she wanted to do was give him a frickin' hug. We do those all the time in my family. They're normal, and there's nothing sexual about them . . . doesn't stop me from coiling my muscles and flinching away sometimes, but that's a matter for another time and place.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Mar 13, 2011 5:51 pm Shinji pretty clearly interprets whatever she's offering him to be inappropriate to their relationship. At that stage of Misato's failures of course pretty much anything is inappropriate, but Misato being Misato and dickchair being dickchair, I'm inclined to think it's something sexual she was hoping for.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Mar 13, 2011 6:15 pm She says "All I can do is this . . ." and then puts her hand on Shinji's shoulder, only for him to flinch away. Call me crazy, but I think I she wanted to do was give him a frickin' hug.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Mar 13, 2011 6:19 pm ^ Ditto -- the primary source for the interpretation is extra-textual commentary. What she does looks more big sisterly than anything else.

[]:             Quote Post by COACH » Sun Mar 13, 2011 6:40 pm There's also the dickchair, which wasn't even in the on-air version of the episode, right? I don't think any of the director's cut material was added for shits 'n giggles.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sun Mar 13, 2011 9:53 pm Misato and Shinji have always been walking a fine line in regards to their relationship with each other: on one side the relationship is a maternal one, on the other side the relationship is a lusty one. Misato may not have been offering sex to Shinji, but that delicate balance that their relationship obtained was in danger of being broken by such an ambiguous action. Shinji, being who he is, was terrified of that prospect and Misato's shame, while not necessarily one of guilt, may be realizing how such actions could be interpreted by a scared teenage boy. There are many ways to interpret that scene and the whole Misato-Shinji dynamic which is what makes it so interesting. Now I've done my fanwanking for the day.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Mar 14, 2011 2:35 am My understanding is that she was offering comfort but really seeking it. Though sex probably wasn't what she was consciously looking for I also don't doubt that it was a highly possible outcome had he not rejected her so coldly. Like everyone else in the series she doesn't know how to open up with others, and her coping methods seem to be 1) keep everyone at a safe distance through the adoption of a persona (much like Asuka does), and 2) to use sex as a shortcut to assumed intimacy without ever really being emotionally available. I suspect he turned away out of shame for his earlier inability to offer her such sympathies, but that's just a guess. I don't buy for a second that he percieved the sexual tension and rejected her advance on that ground.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Mon Mar 14, 2011 3:24 am I agree with the notion that Misato wasn't really (at least consciously) trying to bed Shinji... but is that the point? As far as I can see, they haven't shared many hugs and cuddles up to that point, so whatever physical intimacy they had shared without much emotional one... wouldn't it have just been, like, 'lite version' on Misato's "let's hide behind assumed intimacy" sex? And I am 99% positive Shinji's rejection was because he intepreted it as an offer for sex (or other physical intimacy) and was mostly scared of being close to another, especially since it would have shaken one of his only relationships he didn't feel actually threatening at that point.

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Mon Mar 14, 2011 4:28 am Cody MacArthur Fett wrote: She says "All I can do is this . . ." and then puts her hand on Shinji's shoulder

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Mon Mar 14, 2011 9:20 am toe mash wrote: Actually on his hand :)

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Mar 14, 2011 9:24 am "Is he afraid of women?" in my opinion practically confirms that Misato's aims were rather sexual. The statement doesn't make much sense otherwise

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Mon Mar 14, 2011 11:08 am Xard wrote: "Is he afraid of women?" in my opinion practically confirms that Misato's aims were rather sexual. The statement doesn't make much sense otherwise
